in getting these non-union men to
leave the city Immediately.


For the past six weeks the Master
Builders association has been quiet
ly gathering and marshaling a host
Of non-union men who are told to
remain In the city until the plans
for the overthrow Of the Building
Trades council are complete.


The time seems now to be very
close at hand and the only matter
that stands in the way Of the arch
enemies Of the council is the fact
that non-union masons are very
scarce in the northwest and 1t is
practically impossible to get them
without commencing a system of ad-
vertising so that these men all over
the country will be made aware Of
the fact that a labor war Is to be
started in this city.


Union men claim to have all the
evidence needed to convince them that
the final test Of strength between the
council and the association was to
have come February Is, by which
time the association figured they
would have nonunion builders of
